title: galium boreale var. rubioides
intro: Galium boreale var. rubioides, also known as Rubby-stemmed Bedstraw or a variant of the Northern Bedstraw, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Galium boreale var. rubioides include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested in the summer months, dried, and then used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
